The jj+git colocated repo at `/home/user/local-repo` tracks an upstream remote at `/home/user/upstream.git`. The upstream has 2 new commits you don't have locally. You have 3 local commits on top of an older base. Fetch from upstream, rebase your local commits onto the new upstream HEAD resolving any conflicts, and write a status report to `/home/user/status_report.txt`.
Let me start by exploring the repository structure and understanding the current state of things.

todos.content: Explore the jj+git colocated repo at /home/user/local-repo
Rebase local commits onto new upstream HEAD, resolving any conflicts

cd /home/user/local-repo && jj git fetch --remote origin && jj log --no-pager -r 'all()'

parts.text:
Fetch successful. Now I can see the full picture:
- Upstream has 2 new commits: `upstream-3` (c5065653) and `upstream-4` (0c6e4657) — now at `main@origin`
- Local has 3 commits (`local-1`, `local-2`, `local-3`) branching from `upstream-2` (via an empty commit)

Let me inspect the files to understand potential conflicts before rebasing.

todos.content: Explore the jj+git colocated repo at /home/user/local-repo
Rebase local commits onto new upstream HEAD, resolving any conflicts

input.command: cd /home/user/local-repo && jj show tslyxpoo --no-pager && echo "---" && jj show xyynqpmv --no-pager && echo "---" && jj show wyxwqmzr --no-pager
